title: malvastrum sulphureum
intro: Malvastrum sulphureum, also known as the Yellow Bells, is a flowering plant native to South Africa, characterized by its bright yellow, bell-shaped flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis, coughs, and colds due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The Yellow Bells has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and providing antioxidant properties. Malvastrum sulphureum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to alleviate respiratory problems and soothe coughs.
